dialogue.utterance: Dispatch to Unit 2 for possible pregnancy-related abdominal pain and vomiting at 9300 block Cherryfields Dr in Richmond.
Hi, I'm EMT Janine with Rescue Squad 1. What brought you to call 911 today?
She's a 23-year-old woman in severe pain with cramping abdominal pain, nausea, and vomiting.
How long has the pain been going on, and did anything precipitate it?
She said the pain started two hours ago after eating fish, and it got worse with vomiting.
Ma'am, can you tell me your name and where we are right now?
Yes... My name is Sarah. I don't know the exact address. You're in Richmond.
Ma'am, what is your name and today's date?
I'm Sarah. The date is... October 13.
Oxygen saturation is currently 100% while breathing room air.
Our blood glucose reading is 107 mg/dL, which is within a normal range.
She's breathing at a rate of 20 per minute, even depth and unlabored.
Her heart rate is currently 97 beats per minute.
Blood pressure is 118 over 63 with clear, strong radial pulses.
Airway is patent, clear and self-maintained. No signs of obstruction here.
Breathing is at a rate of 20, non-labored, and of regular depth.
Pulse is strong and regular with no signs of shock. Skin appears warm, dry, and pink.
You're responsive and oriented to person, place, time, and event.
To sum up vitals: pulse 97, respiratory rate 20, BP 118/63. O2 saturation is 100%.
No signs of chest pain or distress; pain is reported to be abdominal.
Can you tell me if you have any allergies or medications currently in use?
I'm allergic to lithium, Bactrim, and Tegretol. I take nothing else, not even prenatal vitamins.
Can you tell me about your pregnancy history?
I was G3P2A0, with both C-sections. No complications including pre-eclampsia or diabetes.
What medical issues or diagnoses do you carry?
I have bipolar disorder, but no other diagnoses. I've had regular prenatal visits.
This pain-does it feel similar to labor pain? Any other symptoms?
Yes. Cramping, very similar to labor. I also vomited my lunch, and it made the pain worse.
What time did you have your last meal, and what did it include?
Fish for lunch, around noon. I haven't had anything after that.
Have you had any changes in your mucus plug or fluid loss?
No fluid leak or bleeding. But I did notice my mucus plug came out 30 minutes ago.
How would you rate your pain out of 10?
It's an 8 out of 10. The cramping is intense, like labor pains.
Can you describe the pain? Sharp, pulling, or cramping?
Very cramping all over and radiating to my back.
Does anything seem to make it worse or better?
Vomiting makes it worse. It's been constant since it started.
Her heart rate has increased to 104 beats per minute.
Blood pressure is now 111 over 72.
Blood glucose is showing 0. Vomiting may be causing that.
O2 saturation remains stable at 100%.
Protocol for pre-term labor appears most appropriate. We'll monitor and get her to the hospital.
Partner, would you start an IV on the left antecubital with a 20G?
IV just started on the left arm with a 20-gauge needle. Saline lock in place per protocol.
Did you do the ECG monitoring as part of the protocol?
Yes, 4-lead monitoring shows sinus rhythm around 90-100, and higher during the vomit episode.
Full ROM in all extremities, and her gait is steady while moving.
She's secured with a five point seatbelt restraint in a position of comfort.
She was assisted up and onto the stretcher, then loaded carefully into the ambulance.
Chippenham Hospital is her destination per choice. We'll transport directly there.
ER nurse received the report with EKG results, IV in place, and vital signs.
The ER was contacted during transport with the patient's history, including pain and vomiting details.
All patient information was turned over, including vitals, pain level, and prior medical history.
She was asked about the time and location in the ambulance and answered correctly.
Today is October 13th, and we're in an ambulance on the way to Chippenham Hospital.
She was safely loaded, covered with a blanket and sheet, and prepared for transport.
She was securely loaded onto the ambulance for safe transport.
IV remained functional and accessible during transport, per protocol.
EKG again shows clear readings with no life-threatening arrhythmias.
Stretcher was repositioned to help her stay comfortable.
GCS is 15, indicating she is fully alert with no changes in orientation.
Pupils are round, equal, and reactive to light. No signs of extraocular movement issues.
Strong radial pulses on both sides. All extremities show good blood flow.
A verbal and written report with IV status, vitals, and allergies was given to ER staff.
She is now in the hands of labor triage at the hospital. No further EMS care needed.
EKG, IV status, and allergies were all explained during handoff to receiving ER staff.
She used the hospital restroom under her own power, thanked us, and was stable.
Recent vitals show BP 111/72, HR 104, and O2 saturation at 100%.
We were able to complete a thorough S.A.M.P.L.E. history and pain assessment. Thank you.
Latest assessment shows stable pulse of 104 with no arrhythmias observed.
All final documentation was done before she arrived at her chosen facility.
ER will take over her care in the L&D triage room for further assessment of possible labor.
She remained alert and oriented throughout transport to the hospital.
Hospital was advised to expect her arrival with a full report already transmitted.
